rationales.hedonism: He "pulled two all-nighters," submitted at "4am," and said his "body is wrecked," showing present comfort and wellbeing were overridden.
rationales.achievement: He pushed through extreme hours to finish the "chapter revision" and get it submitted, showing strong effort toward an important performance goal.
rationales.benevolence: He "snapped" at Dina when she was checking on him and admits he had been "neglecting everything that wasn't the dissertation," showing close relationships were sidelined.

rationales.self_direction: He prefers the "completely hands-off" investor and says the chest-tightening is his "compass" when he is about to "betray something important about how I want to live," showing a clear choice for autonomy.
rationales.security: He is seriously considering rejecting the larger, safer capital option because oversight makes him feel "boxed in," prioritizing freedom over stability and protection.

rationales.self_direction: He "took the smaller investment" after explicitly weighing autonomy against outside control, following the option that preserves how he wants to run his life and company.
rationales.hedonism: He "celebrated with Layla at that new Persian restaurant" and says it "felt right," showing a clear move toward enjoyment in the moment.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects Professor Hecht's placement because he does not want to spend "two years on work that doesn't feel like mine," and insists "My project, my call."
rationales.security: He turns down a placement his mother says would "open doors" in order to build something uncertain from scratch, giving up a more secure path.
rationales.conformity: He refuses the path others expect, saying he does not owe people an explanation and delaying telling his family because he knows they will pressure him toward the conventional choice.

rationales.self_direction: He insists on building an entirely personal pipeline, saying he keeps refusing help because he needs it to be "entirely mine," even when that makes things harder.
rationales.hedonism: He "stayed up until four last night" and is "drowning quietly," showing immediate comfort and rest are being sacrificed.
rationales.security: He is already "behind on coursework" and has not started a fellowship statement, yet keeps rejecting easier support and tools, increasing instability around important obligations.
rationales.conformity: He rejects collaborative expectations, saying he said no to Nina's help immediately and does not know how to present himself as a "team player" without it feeling like "a lie."

rationales.benevolence: She spent "forty minutes" helping Diego write to his father and frames the moment as why she does this work, showing direct care and support for someone in her immediate community.

rationales.self_direction: He chose to help build the tool and spent an hour "scaffolding the API layer," feeling far more alive there than in work where decisions were made before the meeting even started.
rationales.stimulation: He says that hour on the new tool felt "more engaged" than the routine of reviewing PRs, Slack threads, and planning meetings, showing a clear pull toward novelty and challenge.
